meta.title: NFT Ticketing Doesn't Work and We're Selling to Ticketmaster

transcript: So, first and foremost, Happy Breakpoint. I have a little bit of a quiz here for everybody. You know, when we look at every industry, they pay the top dollar for those that accelerate their core engine of business. Tech giants pay their engineers and AI teams. Hedge funds invest in their quants. These are the CFOs of the world's biggest companies. If size and capital determined compensation, you'd assume one of these CFOs would be the highest paid CFO on the planet, right? It's actually this guy. Uh, former colleague of mine. His name is Joe. He is the highest paid CFO in the world. And he doesn't work at a hedge fund. He doesn't work at an AI company. He works at Ticketmaster. So, you're probably asking yourselves, why is an company that sells tickets and entertainment have the highest paid CFO on the planet? This is the truth about Ticketmaster. Ticketmaster isn't a ticketing company. It's a bank. Ticketmaster deploys 10 billion dollars in loans every year to venues, which venues use to pay artists and to finance their deals. They charge fans what's called an interest rate. You're all familiar, right? The service fee. Now, you're asking, Ticketmaster owns these venues? No. They use debt to lock them into long-term deals. And they recoup this debt using ticketing products. But what about the artist? What about Taylor Swift? What about Post Malone? Well, they can't use their own ticketing company because every venue they go into is locked into these type of deals. Like, this is kind of funny when you think about the whole purpose of NFT ticketing, right? For years, we've looked at NFT ticketing and effectively it was collectibles sold to venues who need liquidity. What about resale? Easily bypassed. And the cost of using NFTs as an infrastructure for tickets? Astronomical. It didn't make sense from a business standpoint. Artists and venues need liquidity, not collectibles. So, a little bit about myself. My name is Ahmed Nimale. I'm the co-founder of a company called KYD Labs. We're the world's largest on-chain ticketing company. We service the likes of Led Zeppelin's Robert Plant, Charli XCX, Travis Scott, Dillon Francis. 150,000 fans come across our sites every single month. And I want to say, NFT ticketing dies today. Where NFTs turned tickets into collectibles, our standard TIX turns them into RWAs. Meet TIX Protocol. A living ticket born as a financial primitive. A programmable instrument that allows artists and venues to take loans based on their future ticket sales. All while providing lucrative yields for DeFi lenders. Capital meets culture meets yield. Well, you're probably thinking, this sounds cool. Does it exist? It's live. We have migrated all of our tickets to the from a compressed NFT standard to TIX today. And we'll be powering millions of tickets and tens of thousands of artists uh and more than a billion monthly active users that listen to those artists. Unlike most consumer crypto what we see, we have distribution and it's real today. Now, with TIX, we create a world where every ticket becomes a commodity. This is a private credit use case based on real-world cash flows. But TIX wasn't built for KYD Labs alone. It's a standard that will define ticketing. It's a standard that we developed for not only ourselves, but for Ticketmaster. I'd like to say today, creators don't need coins. Creators need Ticket Capital Markets on Solana. Thank you.

enrichment.generated_query: TIX Protocol KYD Labs Solana Breakpoint 2025 (announcement OR blog OR docs OR documentation)

sources.snippet:
Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform  | Drift Updates
[![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f0866da8eed4c/6867a1f64ca2c75f0d14f371_16c97ce9017b907297827c5113a068f9_Frame%201597880158.avif)](https://www.drift.trade/)
[
Launch App
](#)
## Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform
December 4, 2025
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f08e5798eed50/693023e13e4b72ada17e0fe2_2025-12-03%2017.19.13.jpg)
[
Contents
](#)
[
Contents
](#)
[
](https://twitter.com/driftprotocol)[
](https://discord.com/invite/fMcZBH8ErM)
[
Trade Now
](https://app.drift.trade/)
![]()
![]()
Drift v3 is the protocol’s biggest performance upgrade yet. Rebuilt around trader execution, v3 delivers**10x faster fills, 10x improved liquidity, and a cleaner, more intuitive interface**than v2.
This upgrade was built for one thing: outperformance. Instant execution. Deeper liquidity. A suite of tools that helps you trade faster and with more confidence.
Drift v2 was the first to bring cross-margin and multi-asset collateral to DeFi on-chain trading. We built on Solana to deliver NASDAQ-level performance on-chain and with major Solana upgrades like Alpenglow, Firedancer and Jito’s Block Assembly Market (BAM), Drift is built to outperform as the network scales.
Since launch, Drift has surpassed 1.5B in TVL, $133B+ in total trading volume and $650M in open interest. Drift v3 is built to take those numbers and your trading even further.
**Drift v3 will be available on Dec 4, 2025.**New users will need an invite code to access Drift v3.
**DM**[**@DriftProtocol**](https://x.com/DriftProtocol)**to request access.**
## Why v3?
Drift v3 represents the protocol’s next stage of maturity: the point where years of infrastructure, liquidity, and execution improvements converge into a faster, clearer, more reliable trading experience.
**Over the past year, Drift has evolved in three critical areas:**
1. Execution speed
2. Liquidity depth
3. Trader experience## How Drift v3 outperforms
*The road to v3 was focused on improving liquidity, trading execution and speed. Here’s what that looks like in practice:*
### **Trading Execution and Speed**
* **10x faster order fills:**On Drift v3, 85% of market orders are filled within the same slot. A slot is Solana’s smallest time unit (about 400 milliseconds). On Drift, that means that trades are executed in under half a second. Instant execution.
* **15x faster take profit/stop loss triggers:**For BTC, it dropped from 15 slots (\~6 seconds) to 1 slot (\~0.4 seconds). This means your risk controls and exits now trigger immediately, reducing slippage and missed opportunities.
* **Gasless trading by default:**Unlike other DEXs, where you pay gas on every trade, Drift abstracts gas fees so that’s one less thing to worry about eating into profits
* **400ms oracle updates**: Prices update every 400 millisecond (roughly 2.5 times per second). This keeps quotes accurate and reduces the chance of stale pricing.
* **Scales with Solana:**As Solana increases block size and throughput, Drift automatically gets faster too, handling more trades per second.### **Liquidity**
* **10x reduction in slippage on market orders:**In Drift v2, large market orders could move the price by 20 bps. In v3, that’s been cut to just 2 bps. Traders now get far better pricing, especially on size.
* **5x tighter TP/SL execution:**Take-profit and stop-loss orders are far more precise. On major markets like SOL and BTC, fills are 4x tighter, while non-majors see up to 5x improvement. This means exits trigger closer to the price you set, reducing losses and protecting gains.
* **Introduction of Drift Liquidity Provider (DLP)**: Provides liquidity for both Perps and Spot markets. Community members can deposit funds into DLP to support market-making and earn a share of the pool's profits. Currently in the testing stage. Public launch in Q1 2026.
* **Expanded Market Maker Program:**With v3’s expanded market maker initiatives, long-tail altcoins now hav

sources.title: Kraken to acquire Backed, accelerating xStocks expansion and unlocking global demand for tokenized equities
sources.snippet:
Kraken to acquire Backed, accelerating xStocks expansion and unlocking global demand for tokenized equities - Kraken Blog Kraken Blog
# [![Kraken Blog](https://i0.wp.com/blog.kraken.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/New-Lockups%402x.png?fit=214%2C64&ssl=1)](https://blog.kraken.com/)
[**](#)
[Sign in](https://www.kraken.com/sign-in)[Sign up](https://www.kraken.com/sign-up)
**
[Sign in](https://www.kraken.com/sign-in)[Sign up](https://www.kraken.com/sign-up)
Search for:**×
December 2, 2025|[News](https://blog.kraken.com/category/news)
# Kraken to acquire Backed, accelerating xStocks expansion and unlocking global demand for tokenized equities
The Backed acquisition strengthens our tokenized asset leadership and marks a key milestone toward making global capital markets open, accessible and borderless.
### Share this:
* [Click to share on X (Opens in new window)X](https://blog.kraken.com/news/backed-acquisition?share=twitter)
* [Click to share on Facebook (Opens in new window)Facebook](https://blog.kraken.com/news/backed-acquisition?share=facebook)
* ![](https://i0.wp.com/blog.kraken.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/xStocks-Backed-Annoucement-Blog.png?fit=3070%2C1400&ssl=1)
We are excited to announce we have agreed to acquire[Backed Finance AG](https://backed.fi/), the company driving the issuance of[xStocks](https://xstocks.com/), the leading industry standard for tokenized equities. The acquisition will accelerate the global adoption of xStocks; unifying issuance, trading and settlement to power open capital markets infrastructure for everyone.
xStocks has seen exceptional adoption since its debut earlier in 2025, surpassing $10 billion in combined exchange and onchain trading volume within six months. This acquisition gives Backed access to the capital and resources needed to scale faster and capitalize on surging adoption and growing institutional demand for tokenized equities.
Bringing Backed fully into our ecosystem also allows greater alignment on strategy and investment priorities, unlocking the full economic potential of xStocks.
Together, Kraken and Backed will accelerate xStocks’ expansion, bringing tokenized equities to new markets and extending their reach into everyday financial experiences. The acquisition lays the groundwork for future innovation in tokenized assets beyond equities, advancing our leadership in building open, interoperable financial infrastructure.
xStocks currently offers exposure to more than 60 tokenized equities and ETFs backed 1:1 by the underlying asset. The tokens are live on Solana and Ethereum, with integrations on TON, Tron, Mantle and BNB Chain coming soon.
Investors can trade 24 hours a day, five days a week on Kraken, or directly onchain 24/7, creating a seamless, continuous bridge between traditional and crypto markets. xStocks can be self-custodied and were designed for multi-chain composability, keeping the infrastructure open and usable across the broader ecosystem.
Through the xStocks Alliance, Kraken and Backed have built a thriving ecosystem of partners spanning blockchain foundations, trading venues, and consumer applications. With this acquisition, we plan to further expand this ecosystem, enhancing interoperability and liquidity for tokenized equities across the industry.
**Arjun Sethi, Kraken Co-CEO**: “Integrating Backed into Kraken strengthens the core architecture required for open and programmable capital markets. Unifying issuance, trading and settlement under one framework ensures the infrastructure for tokenized assets remains transparent, reliable and globally accessible. This is foundational work for the next era of market structure. This is bigger than giving people exposure to U.S. equities, it’s about redefining what it means to own assets in the digital era.”
Backed’s team and infrastructure will become part of Kraken, strengthening issuance and compliance capabilities while expanding the reach and utility of tokenized assets beyond the crypto ecosystem

tags.impact_reason: Transitioning from NFT ticketing to RWA-backed lending could bring new capital and utility to the Solana ecosystem, but the existing user base is still being onboarded.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a shattering industrial shackle as the Semantic Anchor because the transcript repeatedly frames Ticketmaster not as a ticketing company, but as a 'bank' that uses '$10B' in debt to 'lock' venues into inescapable deals. The shackle physically represents this financial bondage. The force breaking it—a stream of holographic tickets—symbolizes the liquidity and freedom offered by the TIX Protocol. I included the diegetic text '$10B' stamped onto the shackle to quantify the scale of the problem and anchor the visual directly to the core financial narrative.
visual_idea.description: A colossal, brutalist cast-iron shackle, pitted and heavy, fills the center frame as it violently shatters. Stamped into the dark metal, the text '$10B' glows with a faint, oppressive red heat. From the fractured core, a brilliant cascade of iridescent, holographic concert tickets erupts, composed of flowing data streams and glowing circuit patterns. The scene is captured with a shallow depth of field, set against a dark, hazy industrial background, pierced by dramatic god rays that illuminate the moment of liberation and the digital fragments flying through the air.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A colossal, brutalist cast-iron shackle, pitted and heavy, fills the center frame as it violently shatters. Deeply stamped into the dark, textured metal, the characters '$10B' glow with a faint, oppressive red heat. From the shackle's fractured core, a brilliant cascade of iridescent, holographic concert tickets erupts, composed of flowing data streams and glowing circuit patterns. The monumental scene is captured with a shallow depth of field, set against a dark, hazy, desolate industrial background. Dramatic god rays pierce through the haze, illuminating the violent moment of liberation and the digital fragments flying through the air, creating intense cinematic chiaroscuro lighting with deep shadows and sharp highlights. Volumetric lighting emphasizes the breaking and scattering. Composed for a vertical 3:4 aspect ratio, emphasizing epic height and monumental scale, with substantial negative space at the top, making the shackle feel colossal. Brutalist cast iron textures, intricate holographic circuitry, ultra-realistic, photoreal detail, 8K resolution, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a shattering industrial shackle as the Semantic Anchor because the transcript repeatedly frames Ticketmaster not as a ticketing company, but as a 'bank' that uses '$10B' in debt to 'lock' venues into inescapable deals. The shackle physically represents this financial bondage. The force breaking it—a stream of holographic tickets—symbolizes the liquidity and freedom offered by the TIX Protocol. I included the diegetic text '$10B' stamped onto the shackle to quantify the scale of the problem and anchor the visual directly to the core financial narrative.
visual_idea.description: A super-macro, 1:1 shot focused on the point of fracture. The rough, dark, pitted texture of the cast-iron shackle is shown cracking and splintering under immense pressure. From within the fresh crack, a brilliant, vibrant light composed of abstract, liquid-crystal data streams pours out, contrasting the old, heavy material with the new, weightless technology. The focus is purely on the material contrast and the raw energy being released at the breaking point.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A super-macro, 1:1 shot focused on the point of fracture. The rough, dark, pitted texture of the cast-iron shackle is shown cracking and splintering under immense pressure. From within the fresh crack, a brilliant, vibrant, luminescent light composed of abstract, glowing liquid-crystal data streams pours out, contrasting the old, heavy material with the new, weightless technology. The focus is purely on the material contrast and the raw energy being released at the breaking point. Dramatic, high-contrast chiaroscuro lighting with deep shadows emphasizes the rough Brutalist cast iron texture, creating a stark visual separation between the dark, detailed shackle and the glowing holographic data streams. Volumetric light emanates from the crack, highlighting the raw energy. Symmetrical, central focus with tight macro framing, filling the frame with intricate texture details. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, ultra-realistic lighting, subsurface scattering.

editorial.headline: KYD Labs Unveils TIX Protocol to Tokenize $10B in Event Financing
editorial.standfirst: The 'NFT ticketing' narrative is dead. In its place, KYD Labs is launching a DeFi-powered credit layer designed to solve the liquidity crisis locking venues into Ticketmaster's grip.
key_takeaways: **TIX Protocol Launch**: A new RWA standard turning tickets into financial primitives for private credit.
**$10B Market Opportunity**: Targeting the annual loan volume currently monopolized by Ticketmaster to control venues.
**Immediate Migration**: KYD Labs is moving 150,000 monthly users and clients like Robert Plant to the TIX standard immediately.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
For years, the crypto industry has pitched **NFT ticketing** as a way to kill the scalper and create digital collectibles. According to Ahmed Nimale, Co-founder of **KYD Labs**, that thesis is fundamentally flawed. In a provocative keynote at Solana Breakpoint 2025, Nimale declared the death of the collectible model, arguing that the real stranglehold on the live events industry isn't ticketing technology—it's banking. Major incumbents like **Ticketmaster** maintain their dominance not just by selling seats, but by acting as lenders, deploying over $10 billion in loans annually to venues to secure long-term exclusivity deals.

To break this cycle, KYD Labs introduced **TIX Protocol**, a new standard that reimagines the ticket not as a jpeg, but as a financial primitive. By converting future ticket sales into programmable on-chain cash flows, TIX allows venues and artists to access liquidity from DeFi markets rather than relying on predatory loans from centralized gatekeepers. This shift transforms the ticket from a consumer product into a **Real World Asset (RWA)** capable of collateralizing loans and generating yield for lenders. "Artists and venues need liquidity, not collectibles," Nimale stated, positioning the protocol as a direct competitor to the private credit operations of legacy giants.

The protocol is not a theoretical whitepaper; it is live on Solana. KYD Labs, which claims to be the world's largest on-chain ticketing platform with 150,000 monthly users, has already migrated its entire inventory to the TIX standard. The platform currently services major acts including **Robert Plant**, **Charli XCX**, and **Travis Scott**. By proving the model with significant real-world traction, KYD Labs aims to force a market shift where even incumbents like Ticketmaster may eventually adopt the standard to access more efficient capital markets. As Nimale put it, "Creators don't need coins. Creators need Ticket Capital Markets."
